taskDescription: Drag the correct terms into the blanks to complete statements about general features and joints of the upper limb.
textField:
The upper limb has two layers of fascia: *superficial* fascia (containing fat and cutaneous nerves) and *deep* fascia (wrapping around muscles like a tight sleeve).

The arm is divided into anterior and posterior compartments by the medial and lateral *intermuscular septa*. The anterior compartment contains *flexor* muscles while the posterior contains the *triceps*.

Dangerous swelling within a closed fascial compartment is called *compartment syndrome*, which compresses nerves and vessels and requires emergency *fasciotomy*.

The two major superficial veins are the *cephalic* vein (lateral) and the *basilic* vein (medial), connected at the cubital fossa by the *median cubital* vein.

The carrying angle at the elbow is normally *5-15* degrees in males and greater in females.

The shoulder joint is a *ball-and-socket* joint with the greatest range of motion but is most prone to *dislocation*.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review upper limb general features and try again.
